structured.research_question:
Does 4-week supplementation of C57BL/6 mice with Lactobacillus rhamnosus
GG measurably reduce intestinal permeability via upregulation of tight
junction proteins?
structured.subject: C57BL/6 mice (intestinal epithelium)
structured.independent:
Daily oral supplementation with Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG (vs. vehicle
control) for 4 weeks.
structured.dependent:
Intestinal permeability, measured by FITC-dextran flux assay; tight
junction protein expression (claudin-1 and occludin) measured by Western
blot.
structured.conditions:
n>=8 per group, age-matched 8-10 week old C57BL/6 mice. FITC-dextran
(4kDa) gavage 4h before terminal blood draw. Standard chow diet, 12h
light cycle.
structured.expected:
L. rhamnosus GG supplementation reduces intestinal permeability by at
least 30% compared to controls, with corresponding upregulation of
claudin-1 and occludin.

procedures.name: Daily Oral Supplementation
procedures.intent: To administer L. rhamnosus GG or vehicle control to respective mouse groups daily for 4 weeks.

procedures.name: Intestinal Permeability Assay (FITC-dextran)
procedures.intent: To assess intestinal permeability by measuring systemic FITC-dextran levels after oral gavage.

procedures.name: Tissue Collection and Processing
